reviewsSummary.note: No new reviews spawned - this is re-run after prior synthesis. Fix feature fix-lecture-9-10-content remains pending (not completed). Blocking issue persists.

blockingIssues.description: Lecture 9 and 10 content extracted from wrong source PDFs (CS:APP Computer Systems instead of ACS2906 Assembly Language Programming). Lecture 9 contains 'Memory Hierarchy' (caching, TLB, virtual memory) instead of 'modular programming, recursion, BIOS/DOS interrupts, TSRs'. Lecture 10 contains 'Exceptional Control Flow' instead of 'comprehensive review, exam preparation'.
blockingIssues.evidence: Verified lectures.json still contains CS:APP content: Lecture 9 title is 'Memory Hierarchy (cont)' and Lecture 10 title is 'Exceptional Control Flow'. Fix feature fix-lecture-9-10-content is still pending (not completed).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add semantic content assertions (VAL-CONTENT-*) that verify extracted content matches the feature description, not just structural validation. For example, check that Lecture 9 contains keywords like 'modular', 'recursion', 'interrupt', 'TSR' and not keywords like 'cache', 'TLB', 'virtual memory'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature extract-lecture-9-10 passed 611 structural validation assertions but extracted completely wrong content. The validation-contract.md only checks 'content exists' not 'correct content exists'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify skill scope - the frontend-worker skill appears focused on UI changes (theme toggle, nav items) not content extraction. Consider whether a content-extraction skill should exist or whether AGENTS.md should document the content extraction workflow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple workers (extract-lecture-1-2, extract-lecture-3-4, extract-lecture-5-6, extract-lecture-7-8) reported 'frontend-worker skill not found' and proceeded manually.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for handling PDF source mismatches. Workers reported that Lecture 5.pdf didn't contain expected Logic content (it had I/O and Addressing Modes instead). AGENTS.md should specify how to handle when PDFs don't match expected content.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature extract-lecture-5-6 worker noted: 'Lecture 5.pdf (61 pages) contains I/O and Addressing Modes' - worker correctly used Lecture 6.pdf instead, but this workaround wasn't documented.
